metadata.school_name: University of Illinois Urbana Champaign Law School

reviews.comment: Professor Leipold's lectures clear your concepts while making you laugh. I never bothered to open the textbook, his teachings were sufficient

Professor Leipold is awesome. I was sad everytime the class ended. He gives a lot of hypos to understand the rules, and makes the expectations for the exam clear. I suspect that his exams got easier because I saw a lot of older complaints about how hard his exams were, but I did not feel this way and I don't think my classmates did neither.

Leipold is a such a gift to the world. You will learn so much, all while laughing and genuinely enjoying class. His examples are engaging and memorable. But most of all, he's incredibly caring and treats all of us with such respect: as if we each have something important to offer. Haven't met a single student who doesn't say they love Leipold.

I have not had a professor as good as Leipold, I loved every minute of class and learned so much. He is so funny that even if you're wrong, being cold-called in his class never catches you off guard, and so long as you do any reading, it is an absolute joy. Best professor hands down, the only downside is that he does not provide past exams.

Consider yourself incredibly lucky if you have Professor Leipold (especially in 1L). You can tell that he really does care about his students. His lectures are engaging + funny. You can literally go to his OH and talk about anything-life, law school, worries etc.! There are not enough words to describe what a wonderful professor Leipold is!

Great professor, lectures are very detailed but manageable. Exam was nearly impossible. Hypotheticals on the exam were 50 times harder then what we covered in class. Unfortunate he designed the test that way. Hoping for major curve.

Leipold is one of the best professors at this school. His class is entertaining and his style of teaching is engaging. In his upper level course he will also email you ahead of time to let you know you're on call which is extremely helpful.

Crim Pro: Investigation was my favorite class in law school so far (one semester left). The subject matter is fascinating because it touches on 4th, 5th, and 6th amendment issues. Leipold does an amazing job of relaying very complex information. Exam was tough-multiple-choice, true-false, and two issue-spotters. You really need to know your stuff!

Criminal Procedure: Adjudication is an outstanding class. Leipold is a terrific professor--he goes over the material slowly and in great depth. The day before the final, he held three office hours and explained very complex concepts in an easy-to-understand manner. Exam is closed book and very hard-true/false, multiple choice, and a couple essays.

Leipold actually understands the concept of teaching. Most law professors can lecture for long periods of times on topics they are experts in. Leipold is different. He actually teaches the material. Not to mention he is a leading expert in the field and literally wrote the book on the topic.
Best teacher at the college of law, worth the price of admission.
Really great guy and incredibly knowledgeable. The class is hard but he makes it fun. Great class!

Professor Leipold is a very sharp, very caring professor who  goes well out of his way for his students.  He is excellent at simplifying complex material and he is very patient with students.  Also very, very funny--he is a great story-teller.  His exams are difficult, but fair.
Really approachable and an interesting professor.  He doesn't try and scare students like some other professors, and isn't hung up on his accomplishments.  Enjoyed the class.

Looks sort of like a small child in a big boy suit. Nice guy, and good presentations. Problem is that I don't think we ever went a class without hearing about Justice Powell. We got the point. Ok. Yeah. We got it.
Clearly understands the law and explains it pretty clearly.  Often ends class by spending 15 minutes bragging about himself.  Does not provide practice exams.

Great professor.  I was turned off to crim law by Prof. Moore but wow, Leipold's crim pro class made me realize that it wasn't the subject matter, but rather the professor that makes the difference.  Leipold holds your attention, and is very easy to follow.  Spectacular teacher, cares very much about the class as evidenced by our field trip.
By far the best teacher on the planet. He doesn't get caught up in his own brilliance, unlike a lot of these professor chumps. Super nice, super helpful, and an amazing person.

this guy told us that his job was awesome because of the students.  he is well-versed in the law and makes it easy to understand the concepts.  the only thing i would change is the syllabus.  it would be nice if it was more like an outline.

Professor Leipold is hands down the best professor I have had in law school.  Take every class you can with him.  He is incredibly clear in his presentations, and also a very funny and interesting person when he teaches, and also to talk to after class.
amazing.  loves teaching and students.  probably the best professor i have ever had
No Comments

leipold-approachable,funny;class presentations are served up on a platter;exams are not-gives very challenging exams;stay on top of material;expects you to make connections btwn concepts on exam he doesn't talk about in class;gives tough mc's on exams

The GOAT of UIUC law writing professors. The emperor of ERISA. The terror of Trusts and Estates. Once in a generation talent that you just don't see in this era. People have spent the GDP of small countries to have a chance to play trivia with him at the PILF auction, and word on the street is that it was worth every penny.

The best writing professor at UIUC Law. Would take a full semester of this man's courses if possible. Cares about students and will gladly meet outside of class to discuss papers. He is also a 3x Jeopardy champion and a 2nd place finisher at the prestigious Blind Pig Trivia Night.

Amazing Prof! Gives great feedback, going to office hours is key. One of the nicest and most constructive graders at the law school. Gives you feedback that is actually relevant and easily understandable. Wants to see everyone succeed.

If you want a good grade, You HAVE to go to office hours. Sean and the TAs tell you stuff he doesnt tell the entire class. Really annoying class, but whatever. I wouldnt reccomend wasting so much time and energy on a 2 credit class.

Anderson is as condescending as they get. doesn't give adequate instructions. Any notes puts up on the screen regarding his strict preferences are taken down before you have a chance to note them. Make sure you go to office hours or else you will not get critical instruction that he gives everyone else in private & your grade will suffer for it.

This semester Anderson switched his class to socratic method, but it doesn't take away from enjoying the class. He's a fantastic professor that makes learning about Trusts and Estates as interesting as one could. I would recommend taking any class of his if you get the chance.

Professor Anderson does a good job of preparing those of us who may not love the idea of court room advocacy. He's readily available to help whenever he can and he truly cares about the students he teaches.

What to say about Sean Anderson. This man is an absolute gem. DO NOT buy the book for his class though. You don't need to read it and he essentially never uses it. He's really easy to talk to at office hours and gives great feedback.

Professor Anderson is a solid professor. You DO NOT have read the books he assigns for the class--I would recommend borrowing them since he uses them sparingly. He cancels half the classes, which is nice. He is very helpful during office hours and answers pretty much any questions you have. He spends most time in class going over structural things.
Not good.

Professor Aviram is probably one of the nicest profs you'll find at UIUC. His BA course requires watching some videos before class. In class, he teaches you how to apply the concepts taught in the videos to factual scenarios. Overall, a pretty great professor and a fair grader (maybe a bit on the tougher side).

Puts information on the slides that have no application for the exam. Passes it off as "may be useful in the future". Doesnt have the applicable rules on the slides and instead elects to put the information that isn't useful for us

If you want to save some money then this class is for you, no textbook required. Although you 'll feel miserable bc he does not explains that much during class, is expecting that we see some videos and then solve very abstract and complicated problems in class. No guidance whatsoever. the exam is a take home exam.

Prof Aviram's BA class is different. You don't need to purchase a text book (yay) because he organized the material into (1) online lectures available on YouTube and (2) power point slides used in class, which include a wealth of practice questions. His lectures are very clear - an amazingly smart, kind, and helpful teacher. Will take again.

Prof Aviram reads lengthy power points and talk endlessly instead of going straight to the point. His class is boring, less instructive and more of a show. His final is usually a take home exam and after he distributed arbitrary grades, he still not explain his rationale for grades nor is he persuasive about the legal problem and solution

Just reads off of power point. Does not explain well. Does not seem to care if people show up or learn. Appears to be a good guy that assumes law students will do the work; he is wrong. The material could be interesting and he could convince people to chose this as a field to enter but he does nothing to encourage it.
I don't know how great of a teacher I think he is, but he sent out a picture of his newborn baby to the entire law school and that baby was cute!!!!

Class can be boring at times, but Aviram is a genuinely nice guy and makes class relaxed compared to others in law school.  The exam is not difficult if you prepare well.  Talk to him outside of class if you want to do well--he's great around exam time.
One of the best professors I have had.  A genuine, smart, and all around wonderful person.  His slides are very helpful.  I will take any class he teaches.
I really enjoyed Professor Aviram's BA class.  I recommend him as a professor.  He's very organized, clear in his teaching, and I enjoyed his lectures.

Professor Aviram is very nice in person outside of class. He seems to have all of his lectures memorized and can recite them to you in his office. He speaks very fast, teaches a difficult class, and teaches a large lecture 100+, but he manages to teach well nonetheless. I have never heard of extra credit for class participation though.

Great Prof. except that he talked really really fast.  He is aware of this and slows down when you bring it to his attention.  I don't agree with the comment below saying that participation factors in grading.  I don't know for a fact that it doesn't, but I got an A and didn't say a single word in class.
He is very open to student questions in and outside of class.  His slides were very helpful when studying for the exam as was the availability of past exams.
Professor Aviram is very clear, very nice, very helpful, very good at what he teaches.  His slides RULE.  His extra credit thing did very much result in excessive gunning by a select few.

Aviram is approachable and friendly, and his powerpoint slides are very good.  The one problem is that your grade in his class partially depends on how much you participate, so the superheroes never shut up.
No Comments
He is very open to questions, and very courteous to his students. His use of Power Point is helpful, and it cuts down on note taking.

He friendly and very smart on his topic.  But his extra credit policy encourage rampant gunnerism and discourages others from participating.  He moves a bit too fast and I wish he had explained basic business stuff before starting on the law stuff.

It's alright overall, but grasping guidelines can be a bit challenging. On the bright side, attending her office hours is a lifesaver as it brings clarity. However, the downside is that everyone's flocking to her office hours, resulting in longer waits, especially before the final assignment deadlines. I don't think I would take their class again.

Professor Alam was a fantastic writing teacher. Her class was not easy, but I really appreciated how clear she was about what she wanted. Legal writing can be overwhelming to master without clear guidelines and steps and she took a ton of stress off me by providing these steps. You will know what to expect, so if you work hard you will love her.
Ok class. Lots of work but not in a challenging way. She brings in cupcakes though. Textbooks are required but we barely touched them.
Really solid professor who cares about her students and teaches really valuable techniques.

Alam is the best writing prof here. She's not easy, but she is very fair and makes her expectations abundantly clear. She also actually cares about her students' development. Her slides are very helpful, and she sings to her class and brings snacks.
Professor Alam rocks.

Professor Alam is very kind, very helpful, and always makes herself available to her students. She is my favorite professor. Great professor to help 1Ls through the law school transition, also! Looking forward to her Advocacy class! :)
Professor Alam is an extremely organized professor and she is extremely approachable! Her slides are fantastic. Just follow the writing style, and you'll be fine! Really enjoyed her class!
She was very demeaning and unclear.

Possibly the best professor I've ever had. She is firm, demanding, but fair, and while she expects you to figure out legal writing somewhat on your own, she will support you entirely along the way. Nothing but respect for Professor Schulte, she easily outclasses most other professors here in terms of professionalism, pedagogy, and accessibility.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: It is true that Professor Schulte makes things unnecessarily difficult. She gives vague guidance on your papers, and will not point out simple things like certain criteria being missed on your paper. Instead, she expects you to figure it out on your own. You could get the same experience by just reading the text and submitting memos.

Knows what makes a good brief and memo but goes out of her way to ensure she's not holding your hand in the course to the point of making things unnecessarily difficult. It's one thing to try and teach your students to be independent, it's another to consistently hide the ball.
No Comments

I felt like I did not learn as much as I should have in this class.We wasted time playing games in class a lot.Also,she wouldn't just teach you how to do stuff, you were supposed to figure it all out on your own,and then lose points when you didn't do it right. Grading seemed random too. She is personally a nice enough person though.
No Comments

This was literally THE worst class I've taken in law school. The professor simply talks at you and doesn't really include the readings for class. Moreover, she regularly misunderstands students' questions. But the biggest issue is how disrespectful she is.
A lot of stuff in the reading packet and a lot of digressions in the lectures obscured the law that we were really supposed to be learning for the exam and made the experience unenjoyable
reviews.rating_tags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I really liked Professor Bilz, as well as her lectures which contained a lot of helpful hypos and explanations. As long as you follow closely what she is teaching, those hypos are not that hard to answer. I also liked her reasoning in explaining difficult concepts. Definitely enjoyed her class.
She is obviously brilliant, but she was not a great teacher. But, she is friendly and approachable. Just don't expect to learn much from her in class.

Professor Bilz turns criminal law into a psychology/sociology class. She talks ad nauseum about deterrence rationales. While she talks very little about black letter law. Unfortunately, her exam is substantially black letter law. A good supplement is a requirement if you expect to do well in her class. Because she is not a very good teacher.

Professor Boyle is a very accomplished scholar of international law, who should probably retire from teaching. His online lectures, in my opinion, are very unorganized and too bogged down by stories about how impressive his career was to be actually useful to students interested in this subject. 

Not a good class, only take for credit hours.
smart dude and always predicts things that are current, im enjoying him so far

Nearly all Boyle critics are either fedsoc apologists or can't think for themselves. The man makes it very easy to get a good grade, he gives you one long paper with significant leeway in terms of what to write and how to write it. Also, gunners not allowed. Highly recommend.

Despite his very successful career in international law, he is a horrible teacher. That is a fact. Even if you agree with his political stance there is no discussion or dialogue that comes out of any class you attend. It's just him rambling on about his opinion with nuggets of actually international law teachings. No slides or anything construtive

One good thing about the class is that the final is purely a take home exam that he will give you ten days to complete. Going to the class isn't needed to complete the paper because you can do independent research. This is a great class if you want to get some credit and not have to go to class but the class itself doesn't have a lot of substance.

He has a unique vision on international law. He is skeptical of all governments and dislikes jingoistic nationalism and authoritarianism on all sides whether the US, Russia, Israel, France, England, North Korea, Iran, Saudi Arabia, etc. True believer of self-determination and human rights. His functionalist theory of international law is worthy.

I agree: know what you're getting into. It isn't even "leftist politics" -- it's something else. He dislikes Republicans but also indicts most democrats as being frauds. More of a conspiracy theorist but still a fun, interesting lecture every time. It's worth taking one of his classes in your time at the COL.
this course teaches you nothing
I agree with the poster below, but just know what you are in for before you sign up for it.
Dude is a rock star....this dude is so far out of the box he's on a different planet.  if you don't like leftist politics i would probably avoid though.
sorta like if J Peterman taught a Law School class

I learned nothing in a semester of Jurisprudence, except a lot of tin-foil hat ranting about the Pope and Newt Gingrich. He will use a mic in a small lecture hall, then scream into it the entire class. Expect migraines, and worse-than-1L belittling over questions with no answers. Don't expect actual legal philosophy or history.

Was extremely interested in International Law, but did not learn anything by attending class - had to teach myself everything by reading the book & commercial supplements. We had a paper in lieu of a final exam, which was nice, but every class was like a verbal flogging. He's very passionate and knowledgeable about the subject but too acerbic.

Boyle is one of the few geniuses on our campus and needs to be heard. Discusses issues that MATTER and things that other people are too afraid to talk about or even don't know anything about. Take this class just to hear the speeches..

Boyle spends all class rambling about the evils of America, mischaracterizing his opposition and discounting any class comments. He may make a descent generic LAS 101 prof, but certainly not a law school one where people expect solid, concise arguments.

Boyle is a legend of international human rights. he indicted Milosovic, he won the only two successful genocide cases ever litigated at the World Court.  Conformists dislike him because the facts he puts forward demonstrates how ignorant they are, and exposes their reliance on mainstream propaganda. They prefer teachers who try to be funny.
Lectures most of the class and doesn't believe in Socratic Method since it breeds moral relativism.
Class was a waste of time.  Spent the whole time babbling.

Took Jurisprudence with Boyle and enjoyed it.  He is a very opinionated person, but he is not unreasonable or rude to his students.  Most people have problems with his personal views and unfortunately allow this to unfairly influence their opinion of him.  I found him to be the best read professor at the U of I.  Class was worth it.

Francis A. Boyle (or as he calls himself: "FAB") is a left-wing radical who spent 50% of his classtime name-dropping about what "famous" professors from Harvard and Chicago that he studied under.  DON'T TAKE HIM --- IT'S A WASTE OF TIME.
No Comments

very knowledgeable man;open to questions-need 2-B assertive;not open enough to diverging opinions; his opinions tend to stifle the dynamic of class; kids feel they have to write their papers according to his views for a good grade.  listen-take good notes

Reads from an outline from 2006. Boring and unenthusiastic. Keeps you 15 minutes over time every class. Doesn't take questions during class or does a review session. Worst teacher in higher education I have ever had.

Bru is clear about what he expects you to know... but the test was impossible. He is the nicest guy though, and is a super down-to-earth professor compared to most. Don't be afraid to go to office hours!

Bru is a legendary K's professor at UofI and one of the best in the country. He can be a bit intimidating and his lectures can be extremely dry, however he is really goofy on occasion which makes the class palatable. Overall he's a great professor and I'd be happy to take another course with him.

Great Professor. He makes the subject fun and clear, but he's demanding. If he calls on you and you're not prepared he will not let go for most of the class. He always gives you everything you need to answer in class as long as you did your work. Can be intimidating for some, but has a great sense of humor. The final will be tough.
He is very nice, and in his class you will get a lot.But his final is not easy, you have to prepare for long time.Also, he enjoys the number of remedy of Contract.

Ks without Bru would've been absolutely miserable, but he made the class fun.  Read the material though, because he calls on you and does not relent if you're unprepared.  Great guy, and easy to talk to outside of class.
The Bru not only made Contracts fun, but actually got people (myself included) to _like_ contract law.  Watching Hanson's Umm Bop on you tube at 9:00 in the morning has never been so educational.

Contracts with The Bru = many future contract scholars. High interest class. Socratic method: he's not out to get you, but _read_ the material. He knows. He'll hit 3-4 students per class. Final is Tough. Recommend BLL outline supplement, but not absolutely necessary. He's clear about what he wants you to learn and what he wants on his final.
popular professor.  teaches well and challenges students.  deadpan witty humor.
No Comments
He makes contracts fun.  It is a very dull and boring subject and he makes it awesome.
No Comments

He is a brilliant trial attorney but not a brilliant professor. He is well-respected and deserves it. However, he is an attorney turned professor that has not mastered the intricacies of explaining material, lecturing, or writing exams. The #1 student in our class received a B; several students in the bottom half received A's. Arbitrary grading
Beckett is a great teacher.  He is very approachable and easy to talk to.  If you go to his office hours, he loves to talk and tell you fun stories

Professor Beckett and I never got along on a personal level. We had more than our fair share of disagreements. He was arrogant and authoritarian.  However, it wasn't until I got out into the real world that I realized he taught me more than any three other classes I took in law school combined.

Lives to hear himself talk about his glory days that he calls "war stories" at cost to quality of our education.  Full of himself, and yet not even that intellectual of a professor.  Demands daily attendance, asks irrelevant questions that he thinks are profound/have depth, doesn't teach from the book.  Not recommended, an utter waste of tuition.

Immensely knowledgeable and helpful. He knows White Collar inside out... class is no-nonsense, slightly socratic but not acidicly so. He's an absolute delight if you attend his office hours. WCC is a must before graduation.

Very "real-world" oriented.  He's intimidating at first, but then you realize he's just trying to actually teach you the law as real people use it. We didn't have to read a single case for his class!  woohoo!
too full of himself; not helpful enough.  Class wasn't well planned.  Spent too much time praising himself

Beckett is an exceptional professor.  WCC is one of the best classes I've taken at law school, even though I never had much interest in the subject.  Beckett is fun, clear, and full of practical WCC knowledge.  He knows his stuff, and he'll teach it to you.
Beckett rocks!  I love white collar crime.  Haven't taken the final yet, but his explanations and experience are fascinating.

Arrogance, disorganization, waste of time, and poor work, hurt what could have been an otherwise good course. If you like knowing what you're graded on, don't take this class. If you value your time, don't take this class. If you are smart, you won't take this class.

His disorganization is exceeded only by his arrogance.  He and Gunsales made this class a waste due to their complete lack of organization and inflexibility.  If you value your time, skip this class, because unless they completly overhaul the entire course (not likely given the egos involved) you won't do anything productive all semester.
People that didn't like Bruce Smith are the same people that prefer LeBron over Jordan. They are Un-American and not to be trusted.

Great with the substantive material, but very poor personality. This likely stemmed from being busy, but it sucked that he doesn't care about students beyond fulfilling his teaching duties. One of the rudest profs I've ever encountered in trying to get help outside of class. In class, asking questions is not advisable. He hates that.
BOSS
Excellent professor. Probably the best lecturer at U of I. Extremely intelligent and articulate. Has high expectations and can be intimidating but he means well.

Should teach a clinic about how to be a law school professor. All people who complain about legal education - case method, socratic method, etc. - need to take a class with Smith and see how it's done

Fantastic teacher. Probably the best actual teacher I had in law school.  Only rude if you deserved it.  Took different positions just for the sake of argument/education.  Crystal clear in what he wanted you to know.
Fabulous.  One of most challenging, engageing and dynamic professors any student could ask for.  He will hold you to high standards, but that's a good thing.

Great teacher. Intimidating and scary at first, but you get used to his fast talking speed and sarcastic remarks. Always learn something and feel intelligently and mentally challenged and drained after each class. He's most clear about his exam and grading process. Nice person in office hour. DON'T ask questions during class. Only he gets to do it
good good stuff

Prof. Smith was the highlight of law school for me. He is brilliant, and I never left class without feeling challenged and having learned something. He is a very fair and actually very kind and funny person. Only those students who didn't respect him or others, or who didn't prepare were chastised by him (and rightfully so).

The poster above who said "I am not a wimp about this stuff?" Yeah. Sorry. You are a wimp. Smith pushes his students, but he has more respect for them - and for the teaching process - than just about anyone I've ever met. He's got an edge, but the only people who ever really saw it were those who didn't prep for class...

Absolutely fantastic. One of the funniest people I've ever met - he has the dry Monty Python sense of humor. Sometimes his points are lost in the conveyance but he's so entertaining you don't mind listening and figuring it out. Fabulously fair exam - nothing unexpected. Great outlook on law and life. Pleasure to take a course from!
The man is just awesome.
he is the man

Smith will demand a lot from you but will NOT be condescending unless you deserve it.  If someone gets confused, he is nice about.  But if they don't prepare, watch out.  He is an amazing professor, very prepared, brilliant, and challenging.  And actually very helpful and nice if you make an effort in his class.

Smith is a great lecturer. However, he is rude and condescending. And no, I'm not a wimp when it comes to socratic method. At Illinois, most the professors and the students are respectful of each other. Smith is an exception to this.

This was my favorite professor my 1L year.  He's big into law&econ, and if he calls on you, you better be prepared to defend your statements.  He prepares meticulously for every class and really knows his stuff.

This was the best class I have taken in law school. Smith is a challenging and driven professor and the subject matter of this course covers his own research areas. If you enjoy history, you'll enjoy this course. Some people find him harsh and overly demanding - get over it. He's playing the law school game, enjoy it while he's still at Illinois.
Dedicated teacher - - substantial classroom expectations, but if you prepare, it is well worth your while.  Discussions are very interesting.
No Comments
excellent professor who demands the most out of his students.  initially, comes off as a cold person but after a while you realize he's just trying to be a great professor.

Great professor.  Just relax and listen to what he has to say and you'll be fine - people get too worked up over minor discrepancies in the book.  Nicest guy at the school.  You are in good hands for Torts and Crim.

He was really funny, but not in a way that most people understood. Confusing because classes are so easy enough for middle schoolers to understand, but test were graded extremely hard. Can't believe I ended up with a B
funny and doesnt take himself too seriously.  no interest in humiliation
Overall just a really nice guy who has a big interest in what he actually teaches about.
No Comments

Sweet guy. The class itself is pretty boring, but very easy. I really like Professor Bell. He can be really funny sometimes. He might not remember your name, even by midterms week, but he's nice! The only downside - papers due on saturday nights.
Prof. Bell is probably the best professor I've had in college.  He is extremely nice and really cares about people. I would recommend anyone to take one of his classes.
WORST PROFESSOR EVER!  Her laziness is shocking for a law professor.
Thankful she visited SLU
fantastic.  u of i needs her back.

Business Associations at SMU. Extremely unorganized. Unprepared & wants students to teach. Slides are irrelevant/unhelpful. Unclear expectations. 4 cred class felt like 8 cred class via difficulty & amount of work. Exam questions are unrelated to lectures. GPA killer unless you put your other grades on the line for her class. Prepare to self teach
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: She taught Kaplan Bar Review.  The worst 6 hours of my life.  She speaks to the camera like she is speaking to children.  And skips important and relevant topics with cheeky remarks.  Further, she says the word okay 3 times a minute, at least...Sometimes she will end a sentence with "ok" and begin the next with "ok."  TERRIBLE!!

biggest waste of time so far in law school.  She dumbs things down in class and then expects you to understand and apply complicated concepts that she never taught on the exam.  The casebook was also absolutely terrible.
Awesome.
She just can't teach!!
She is more interested in entertainment than teaching. She will never give you a clear answer on a question. She likes to hide the ball. She likes to make you feel like crap.
She can be difficult to pin down, and can dart from topic to topic. She is very very nice and friendly, though, and can be very helpful.
Funny, easy to understand, enjoyable classes.  Not super socratic, hard multiple choice final.
Super nice and funny. Sometimes moves a bit slowly, and as a result gets behind on the syllabus. Final was mostly MCQ's and a few short essays. The material sucks, but she makes it bearable.
No Comments
The best professor at UIUC Law. Thought admin law would be boring, but pretty much my favorite class I’ve taken here. Great sense of humor.

Humorous, professional, and responsible. I took 2 courses from him, administrative law and federal court. His courses cover a lot of legislative history which is what I liked because it told me where did those rules come from. But you need to be well prepared before classes otherwise you could get lost very easily. The best professor I ever met.
Really, really hard class & topic generally. His exams are not easy either, but he is very fair, organized, & runs a great class. Great if you want to learn a tough but important area. Very legit.

Fantastic Professor, very conscientious and clear in explaining every last detail. Very difficult, however - class requires lots of memorization on top of difficult concepts. Nice guy who is always available. Also humorous and entertaining.

Liked him. Kind man. I remember all those case names because of his slides. He really likes to see students during office hours. We had lunch with him and got to talk to him for two or three hours. Answers all questions.

Professor Colombo is by far the best professor I have ever had in law school. He teaches you every single thing you need to know, makes it interesting and relevant, and makes very difficult topics extremely clear and understandable. Fair exam and fair grading. It does not get better than this folks...
strang points of teaching and I really cannot understand why he shouts a lot...

Colombo is great. Material may not be fun but he explains it thoroughly. Took him for income tax and tax exempt organizations. In both classes he was very clear on the material, and exams were both VERY fair without any surprises. Likes to repeat himself a lot in class but it helps emphasize what you need to know. Definitely take his classes!

Colombo is the best. He is a great professor and an overall nice guy...his class is not easy by any stretch of the imagination though, he really challenges you in class to make the arguments both ways and if you don't think about the material and reading prior to class you will be lost. His exam is extremely fair no surprises.

There's no doubt Colombo is a great professor, undoubtedly one of the best at the law school.  He's extremely clear and easy to understand.  The downside, however, is that he's full of himself.  Not enough space to go into details, but several incidents make that apparent.  I guess most people here, being law students, just don't notice or care.

I've had Prof. Colombo for a couple of classes.  He is by far my favorite professor at the college of law.  Lectures are great, he's engaging, smart, and very clear in his teaching.  A very nice person and always willing to help outside of class.
Contracts with Colombo is amazing! I would take any class he teaches in the future
He is clear, funny, and so nerdy it's adorable. I love him! I am taking his classes every chance I get. They are super useful too.
Amazingly clear and concise. Takes a very difficult subject and helps law school monkeys understand. This is a MUST TAKE class, even if you are not into tax.
Colombo definitely makes the material easy to understand.  He'll go way out and you'll wonder why he's talking about what he is sometimes, but he reigns it all back in and makes it easy to swallow.
Colombo is a master of the material, and explains it clearly and simply.  He tells you everything you need to know to do well.

Prof. Colombo is by far my favorite professor.  I've had him for contracts, tax, and tax exempt orgs.  He makes everything interesting and fun.  He's always clear and concise, and extremely respectful of students.
Interesting guy who loves a good argument, but usually shrugs them off with an "I don't know and I really don't care"
Professor Colombo is extremely enthusiastic and down to earth.  He makes income tax really fun, and is a genuinely good person and teacher.
nice person. talkative though.
Colombo is THE best professor at the U of I College of Law.  Has a very open-door policy (as most profs do) --- I'd highly recommend taking him for Income Tax in addition to Non-Profits.
The perfect law professor.
He is the best prof at the College of Law, hands down.

I was not looking forward to this class, but Colombo made it one of my favorites.  Takes difficult concepts and explains them in a way easy to understand even for non-finance/accounting types like myself.  The material is tough, but he does a phenomenal job teaching it and I learned a lot in this class.
Yay, Professor Colombo!
Very enthusiastic, accessible, always willing to help.
Definitely made class easier and the material a little better...
Great at explaining complex concepts
He follows the same final format every single year.  Class material was very clear and grading was fair.  Very funny!  I thought he could have changed up his examples- too many BMW stories
fabulous! a teacher who likes teaching
Awesome Professor
Good professor.  Easy to understand and entertaining classroom presentation.
Colombo is my favorite!  If he taught all my classes, I would be very happy.  If you get really confused by the reading, you can rest assure that he will make it all better.

I thought I would hate tax before the semester started, but Professor Columbo makes it sooo easy to understand, and is funny, respectful and really interesting, so tax has turned out to be my favorite class this semester!
He makes sure everyone understands before moving on to the next topic and does an excellent job explaining complicated topics.
Good Prof.  Solid Teacher.

Yes, his voice will wake you up in the morning! He's the best professor I've had so far and I wish all my professors were like him.  The class was never boring and I felt like I understood everything... unlike some professors.
he talks a little loud, so you don't have to sit in the front - in fact, maybe you shouldn't, b/c he will hurt your ears, but he is an awesome professor and quite funny too.
excellent professor...practical approach to compelx material...'01 graduate of law school...his courses (corp. tax and income tax) very useful in corp. law setting

Professor Tabb is a wonderful Professor whom everybody loves. He is extremely knowledgeable, respectful and genuine. Attending his bankruptcy class is an unmatched learning experience. Classes are very clear and organized. Favorite Professor of all.
Professor Tabb is extremely organized in lectures and he gave fair exams. He is very kind and he is my favorite professor.
Genius professor, interesting class. But never never never NEVER trust a man with a mustache.

Having a professor this dedicated to his students and passionate about teaching is rare at this level. Not only is he an absolute genius in his field but he goes above and beyond to help students. His exam was an intense 4 hour marathon but it was fair and he graded every exam in about a week and gave grades out early, which is a HUGE relief.

Very useful material. Funny guy. A bit too smarmy and pleased with himself sometimes. However, it's pretty funny most of the time. Has a great moustache. The entire course is multiple choice, which is pretty rad for me.
horrible
Humorous in class. Doesn't usually dwell on one person for too long and will ask brief questions of students at random to keep them engaged and on their toes. Straightforward discussion of material.

Tabb's tests are very difficult to complete in time (hardly anyone does), but they are usually pretty straightforward.  He keeps class interesting, and is always quite boisterous.  30 years ago, he probably organized gunner bingo.

I thought Tabb was good.Class was organized very well, and Tabb is very clear when explaining things.Uses fun examples and hypos.Encourages class participation and never treats you like your completely retarded.Test was VERY straight forward. Look for a remedies question defintely.
Tabb is awesome!! A lot of work, but the class was truly awesome. The dynamic of the class with the prof and the TAs was amazing, lot of fun. I'm going to miss this class

Tabb is a boring teacher;not dynamic or engaging;He gets the concepts across just fine;seems concerned with students really learning;invites Q's;test aimed at major concepts in class-definitely manageable;out of class interaction with students-marginal

This class is relatively easy. The major concepts/rules you need to learn are found in the cases. In class she reinforces some of the main concepts you need to know. If you create a good outline you should do fine. The MC on the exam is a little difficult but the essay questions are very straight forward and fair.

Does not teach, class is just for about tangential discussions.  She has literally said that explaining the materials is "not her job" in class.  There have even been moments of open dissent, where students have raised their hands and asked her to stop wasting everyone's time.  Do NOT take BA1 with her.

I agree with the insecurity part. However, Professor Williams is very knowledgable and clear. She helped me learn torts for sure and it helps me to this day so no complaints there. However, she does seem insecure and whines a lot. I think some normal activities in her life may help...about as exciting as Nixon at the beach. Just read the material!

Professor Williams knows her stuff and is very articulate.  Typing what she says is like reading from a well-organized book.  However, she is also very emotional and insecure, which is reflected in the  condescending way in which she sometimes addresses the students.  This made often made class unbearable.  She needs to get some perspective!

Civil Procedure at Georgetown Law. Some people found him funny, but I thought he was offensive. He made jokes about people becoming alcoholics because of their mothers. I didn't think it was funny. He didn't seem to care about his students. His exam was almost all policy. I have to admit that he knows the material well.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough Grader--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: I took Professor Hyman in Georgetown Law.  His class differs greatly from other CivPro classes.  Most other professors focus on the technical aspects of the rules (it IS a procedure class, after all), Hyman focuses on the why.  Why was this rule made? Why did the lawyer bring the case in this court? Once you get that part straight his class is easy

Professor Hyman is a gentleman and a wonderful professor. His lectures are extremely clear and organized, and he is very respectful in answering students' questions. His office hours are also extremely helpful, and I often find myself communicating with him rather than simply being told the answer. He is definitely a first-class Professor.

Sometimes it seemed like he really enjoyed making students feel stupid in class.  On the other hand, he's obviously brilliant.  Teaching was always clear and organized, even if it wasn't always interesting.
amazing professor with full of patience, clear explaining and helpful way

Hamilton is the man.  The class is broken down so that every aspect fits into neatly made categories that he sets out in the beginning of the year.  He really makes sure that everyone understands the material and is familiar with what will be on the exam.  Best professor I had this year.
I had him for property and legal history, and he's fantastic!  If you have any choice in the matter, don't graduate without taking his classes.  You'll be happy you did.

AMAZING TEACHER!!! Seriously, one of the best teachers I have ever had.  He is INCREDIBLY enthusiastic, hilarious, and discusses things so thoroughly that any confusion you could possibly  Con Law will be completely cleared up. He will tell you what is on the test and TEACH YOU EVERYTHING you could possibly need. Everyone in my section loved him!!!

I like professor Kordik as a person. However, I did not really gain much from her classes other than what I had to teach myself. The room will be easier since she doesn't do much of cold calling, but you will be teaching yourself everything in the class.

I am disturbed that a professor who writes a final exam with TONS of errors, year after year, can sneak by. Not even to mention how little we learned this semester, and how much of the material she ran out of time to cover. The ABA would be HORRIFIED if they audited this course.

Kordik is the worst professor I've ever taken. Her lectures are unbearably boring. She has a weak grasp of the materials. She regularly misunderstands students' questions. All her written materials are full of typos and substantive errors, even her final exams. The only upside is her final is multiple choice, which I do better at than essays.

Kordik is honestly an underrated professor. I took her for both Civ Pro and PR. Both subjects are dry, and there's really not much she can do about that. She's a decent professor, pretty funny, and very chill. No cold calls.
A solid professor. She has a lot of litigation experience and she cares about whether her students understand the materials. Recommended!

I liked Kordik. The final exam was hard--she covers a lot of material in the class that does not appear on MPRE practice exams, be warned! She has six "reflection papers" which are two double-spaced pages where you give your opinion on a certain situation that implicates legal ethics. She doesn't cold-call, and readings mostly aren't necessary.
reviews.rating_tags: Skip class? You won't pass.--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--Hilarious

reviews.comment: It's a mandatory class because no one would take it otherwise, that is not her fault. She does her best to make it interesting and she is nice and helpful. Do a crossword if you're bored, we're all stuck in this together.
Kordik has so much wisdom from all her years of pratice. She was an amazing civil procedure teacher. Very pratical course. Moved fast. All business. Challenging. Brilliant teacher.
Ugh. Really dumbs down class and entertains useless and often ignorant comments. Encourages gunners and doesn't test based on material covered in class.
Floods you with information during class, but otherwise good quality.  Explains things very well, makes jokes throughout the year that helps lighten the atmosphere.

Class moved slowly and was a bit tedious (though, that could have been the subject matter).  Prof. Kordik was very clear and did nice recaps.  There was a lot of class discussion and she called on everyone, even a gunner who repeatedly had only negative things to say about the ADA and were just trying to get a class participation grade boost.

The subject matter is kind of boring.  But she explained it well. Test was harder than I thought and had some ambiguous questions.  Class discussions were kind of boring, but she had good hypos and good explanations.
Upbeat attitude.  Nice.  Interesting hypos.  Provided clear explanations.
Great professor.  A little methodical, but worth the time to get to know.
Boring subject, but a necessary one and she does a great job breaking it down so that it is very clear and easy to understand.  Exam was fair and straightforward.  Encourages class discussion.
This class was ridiculously boring and disorganized and repetitive. So much unnecessary info.
No Comments

She practically gives you all of the answers! Each class starts with a review of the material previously covered.  Prof fosters class discussion and is fair in grading.  She counts class participation in grading.  Teaching can sometimes be boring.
Great Professor, very methodical but clear.  Explinations are so sharp it doesn't matter if you have read.
Subjective grading

Freyfogle is a wealth of knowledge. If all you're interested in is black letter law, take a different class. But, if you're interested in policy discussions and hilarious and highly informative tangents, then you will love his class. Students should check out his articles and books - he's a great writer.

Freyfogle's classes are interesting and informative, as long as you do the reading then you will learn something. If you're not interested in wider issues then don't bother turning up - you're probably boring. He is prone to tangential discussions, but these are commonly very interesting. All-in-all a great professor who has plenty of time for you.
if you like enviro. law - - transfer.

i am not sure if 'incomprehensible" describes what this man brings to the table in class each day.  what a waste of time.  go talk to some random homeless person in the park, you'll learn as much about the law from him or her.

I've done 11 reviews and this is my first negative one.  In all honesty he seems like he may be senile.  He just talks about what he wants, and there is very little, if any, teaching involved in his classes.  He's nice enough, but in a crazy Uncle sort of way.  I got an A, but if I hadn't I probably would be writing a much angrier review

I'm sitting in this class right now.  Class ends in 14 minutes and we still have not discussed the assigned reading.  Last week we ended class early to attend a talk, only he rambled on so long that we had to show up to the talk late.  The guy is full of himself and never shuts up.  Seems easy enough though.
The class is one enormous tangent.  He spent an entire class describing what an acre is.  If you want to do well, expect to learm the material on your own.
Wonderful professor.  Beautiful essayist too.  Nuanced lectures, a nice break from the ordinary. A fascinating man, a great teacher.  Take anything you can from him.
He uses the same tangents in all of his classes!  He's a great guy, but his teaching is sooo boring
so boring.  droned on and on and on
No Comments

Terrible. Worst professor I've had in law school. Etienne seems completely disinterested in the subject she's teaching. Lectures are hard to follow and she wastes a lot time calling on people who are not prepared. She is not good at answering questions. Worst of all, she was terrible at explaining the differences between common law and the MPC.

Worst lecturer ever. Shes not confident in the material, and during racial discussions, she coddles the white fragility even though shes black. Its incredibly frustrating. She also sounds like a robot when she speaks.
Too experimental.
Difficult to comprehend.  Too concerned about morality vs. legality.  Does not seem to be particularly inspired by teaching.

You are on your own! Get supplemental books and learn everything yourself because following her lectures/explanations will land you in a mess of conflicting information. She's very nice but seems too busy to look anything up and instead gives information off the top of her head that is often wrong. Doesn't show up to office hours or reply to email.

Etienne was awesome. She's hilarious and has a very dry sense of humor. Take her out to lunch and I guarantee you will have a blast. She makes class fun and her exam was totally fair. Anyone who thought they were going to be tested on philosophy is a moron, she made it clear from day one what would be tested.

I think professor Etienne was a good teacher. Yes she can be confusing at time but that is only because she doesnt spoon feed 1L's the answer and makes you think for yourself. I really liked her teaching style and I learned to most and remembered the most in her class. The multiple choice was really hard but the hypo was very fair.
Her major problem is that she teaches one thing and tests another.  Why have a philosophical class, an then only test the MPC and common law.  Class just didn't make sense.

A very nice person...outside of class. Inside of class she was difficult to understand and she did not truly understand the material she was teaching. This is probably a result of the fact that she is more focused on her research then she is teaching. She lets people talk for way too long, I ended up teaching myself the whole semester.
Subject of discussions were very loose and disorganized.  As a person who is now in criminal law full time, it is amazing how little she taught me.  Better to use just black letter outlines.

Outside of class, she's really nice, but she's just not a good prof. There was too much opportunity for students to share irrelevant opinions and thoughts. She was rarely able to answer questions effectively, mostly because she didn't appear to know her subject very well. I taught myself the class from the text/supplement.  The final was fair.

Nice person, but did not seem to like teaching! I like the Socratic method, and she did not make good use of it- calling on someone who doesn't read and letting them dig a hole for 20 min, is not good for learning. Boring. Use black letter outline but also study from class notes.
Not bad I guess.

I thought she was great!  Sure, she doesn't use much socratic method (only to begin class), but it's nice to have a break from the fear of other classes.  And unlike some other profs, Etienne Was Actually a Real Trial Lawyer for a number of years.  She's smart, very friendly, and fills her classes with fun tidbits about actual Trial Practice.
Terrible.  Terrrible.  Terrible.  Really the worst professor ever.  There was open dissent in the class by the end of the quarter.  Awful.

I had her when she visited at the University of Chicago and she was TERRIBLE - the absolute worst teach I have ever encountered. She didn't know the material and couldn't have cared less about the course. She gave no assistance in preparing for exams and was weeks late returning them to us. I would NOT RECOMMEND!

Liked her personally but she does not seem to care about teaching. She did not know the material well and spent almost all of our class time picking on one girl who never read rather than actually teaching us anything.
Seemed disinterested in teaching our class.  Also did not know the MPC well which left us feeling very unprepared for the exam.  Was not availiable or approachable.
Professor Etienne takes class too slowly.  Children and the Law could have been really interesting, but instead it ended up being boring and disappointing.

We moved very slowly and didn't cover a lot of ground.  The classes weren't captivating and many of us felt unprepared for the final because it seemed like the MPC information that we needed to know kept changing.

I personally liked her.  Her teaching style was fine, although I think part of the problem is in making Crim Law and Crim Pro two seperate classes, it makes things move slow.  I think her teaching would better be served if these were rolled into one course.

Professor has a poor understanding of the subject matter, and is extremely unhelpful in answering questions (mainly because she doesn't know the answer!). Use Understanding Criminal Law, and good luck! I learned nothing in the lecture.
Her expectations were unclear, and she didn't really lead a discussion - she just let people talk at length. Didn't cover much information.
I don't know if I think she is a good prof.  The class moved way too slow and prof did not really add anything to the material.  Exam questions were fair.  Class was disappointing
Teaches the material well. The textbook is an easy read. Cold calls, but is nice about it. Closed book exam. Fair grader. Must do the practice problems.
Compassionate and caring. Professor Johnson is the best!
Excellent Professor

Johnson was an outstanding Evidence professor. He gives you a workbook filled with problems that he will go over in class, so that helps you prep for the final. He is highly accessible, and since we finished the class early, he made the last classes open office hours. Evidence is tough, but he does a pretty good job of explaining the concepts.

Johnson is a great professor and very easy to talk to.  He was clear in discussing the material.  Like the poster below said, it can be confusing because you bounce between the model penal code and common law, but he does a good job.  He's not there to trick you--he really wants you to learn and understand the material.  A++

Johnson was one of my favorite professors 1L year. The material is confusing, because of the dichotomy between common law and Model Penal Code, but he does his best to explain the concepts clearly. Exam was a little tough-three questions that were sort of like issue-spotters and a reasonable multiple choice section. Very helpful during office hours
My favorite professor at U of I. My only complaint is that he doesn't teach more classes.

Evidence Expert. The exam was extremely similar to the in class hypospractice workbook we do everyday, so keep up with those and there will be no surprises. Closed note, so much confrontation clause. Likes to cold call.

Amazing professor. Very intelligent and very respectful. Answer questions clearly and in a timely manner. Very analytical in his approach to criminal law and teaches what everyone should know: how to read and apply the statute.

Professor Johnson is the best!  He brings a ton of real world experience to the classroom.  He was so nice to everyone in class and was super available.  If I was more interested in anything related to criminal law, I would definitely take more classes with him.  The exam was rough, but really fair.
unethical
Painfully tedious & boring subject, but Prof. Grant was great.
good summer class
Enjoyable Decedents summer class.  Fun, interesting, and final wasn't bad.
No Comments
No Comments
No Comments

After reading some of these posts I think a lot of them are very fair. That said, many are from 2018 and older. I think his tough attitude has turned into more of an abrasive and sometimes rude professor. He is extremely smart but will not be taking him again.

Easy & interesting intersession class. If you don't like labor law, this is the best labor law class for you. He will go off topic a lot, embrace it. He will also look for people to respond rather than cold call. It will get awkward if no one answers. Just answer. The questions are not hard.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: I took labor and employee rights with him. Can be very abrasive, but it's never personal, and if you can adapt to it then he's one of the best profs at the school. He's upfront about both the black letter law and the reality in practice. If you're interested in labor/emp, he is more than ready to help you. Just don't skip a reading.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Everyone should take him. He's one of the most accomplished professors here, but people avoid him because labor is a dying field and because he is tough. He demands a lot, but I'd rather have a professor who pushes than one who slacks off. I have no interest in practicing labor, but his lectures kept everyone engaged/interested.. and terrified.

Prof. Finkin is the most underrated professor in the entire law school.  People are scared of his classes because they are challenging.  Guess what, law school is challenging.  So quit taking classes you can sleep through and take a test at the end of the term and take a class where you just might learn something.
No Comments

Finkin is probably the most entertaining professor you will ever have.  He is sarcastic and crass.  Class was always incredibly humorous and equally terrifying.  Be prepared.  Read a lot.  Look at supplements because he flies by.  It is a very difficult and tough class, but he makes it completely worth it with his kiss my ass attitude.
This guy is flat out rude.  Smart but arrogant.

Poorly structured, assignments are more redundant than helpful. Massively long guidelines for assignments, super difficult to follow along. Really big waste of time, please do not take, not what I expected at all.
Take this class and ur so cooked. Waste of time

easier than my engineering classes, assignments aren't difficult just require reading. some videos are outdated but it's a good way to learn about more areas of UX. needs more actual creation of designs but many chances to learn new tools on your own.
Dont take it. You will regret it
terrible class, weird teacher

Class isn't even hard content wise assignment are just written vaguely and are very long. Main trouble I had was that grading can be inconsistent like I felt it won't always follow the rubric to a T sometimes there will be points taken off for things not explicitly stated (or really mentioned at all) in the rubric.
Explores a lot of connections to UX with big projects. No group work anymore. You have to show up and can't just do everything with AI but it's not that hard.

This class was more of an overview than expected. It's mostly homework projects and no big tests.  There's a lot of flexibility and choice on assignments but they're long. I liked the making the user flow, figma prototype and scratch game. It's a good class if you're new to coding and like doing projects with art or if you want to try AI.
dont take this class bro ginger doesnt know anything

Good guy, he cares, he's passionate. People giving him a 1 over wordy descriptions in the HW is not fair. Put forth effort and Professor Ginger will do what he can to support.

I wish email communication was more open, but in general, good caring professor.

Course is disorganized and grading policy is unclear. The assignments are long and tedious so be prepared to be working on homework for around 3-7 hours a week (maybe more). Participation is super strict in this class, so be prepared to talk often. There's a group project the end. Please do it with someone you know. Will save you:)Enthusiastic guy!
very inexperienced, disrespectful, not emphatic, plays cool and hard, it was such a burden. never take this class from Jeff.

One of the worst experiences I've had in a class. The assignments are entirely too complex to understand. He writes assignment instructions as a short essay. I put my best time & effort into assignments and that wasn't good enough. I emailed him once but he said I was "asking the right questions". Grading was incredibly unfair and picky as well.
reviews.rating_tags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Unfortunately, this class burdens students with weekly unclear, dense project assignments. Attendance strict, late work penalized heavily. The workload is excessive, projects overly difficult, lacking practicality, hindering learning. Jeff seems quite nice, but class is unstructured with complicated/confusing instructions and is unaccommodating.

Ginger makes class unnecessarily difficult. He claims he has taught this class many times but calls us "guinea pigs" every week. He doesn't even know how to set up the Canvas or the Zoom. Although lecture is not necessary, difficulty comes in the contradictory hw instructions and stubborn policies. Does not seem knowledgable about what he teaches.

She is probably a better professor than she was dean (because her decision of dismissing me from the law school in 2006 likely cost the State of Illinois billions of dollars (likely opportunity cost of my productive capacity as opposed to being treated as primarily a mental health patient in some University of Illinois experiment). Enjoy Australia.
Loved her for Torts!

She is enthusiastic, but she focuses too much on philosophy to the point where it gets pretentious. Also, I agree with the previous posters that she does have favorites, I thought we are in law school, not middle school.

Professor Hurd was one of the most engaging professor I have ever had, and she is brilliant. She takes a lot of time out of the classroom to work with students and did so much for us. The final, however, was the hardest test I've ever taken and she really threw us a curveball there, and many of us felt like we didn't have time to finish.
I LOVE crim with Professor Hurd. She's the most engaging professor I've had as a 1L. She's extremely caring and makes a lot of time to be available to help students.

Professor Hurd is an amazing individual with a great sense of humor. I definitely plan to take classes with her in the future if I'm able. There's never a boring class and she always gives out outlines for every subject of what you should know which is helpful.

Super kind and easy-going person. Treats you like a colleague as much as a student. There is some hypocrisy in a class that is dedicated to environmental awareness that also give single-sided color printed handouts nearly every class. Lots of movies. You are guaranteed to learn something. Beware of some extremist environmental propaganda.

Professor Hurd is a wonderful Professor and she is an extremely kind-hearted person who always tries her best to not make students feel stupid. She likes encouraging students and her lectures are extremely organized. She is also very dedicated to our learning and even held a whole-day Q&A session before exam this semester. Absolutely love her!

She can be hard to follow in class, but the idea that she teaches "philosophy" instead of the law in 1L classes shows only that you miss the point of the class & the law, in which the reasoning behind a rule is just as, if not more, important than the rule itself. Also, she gave me an A for a paper obviously disagreeing with her viewpoint???

Be careful. She favors students over others. This is not a good person. Don't get on her bad side. She loves to wield power and is the definition of unethical. An easy A if you agree with everything she says. I would choose another class if you have the option. This woman is pure evil.
Obfuscates the subject a bit as most top-tier law professors do, but appears to be a well-intentioned person.  Would take her class again, but probably will never get the opportunity.

Such a sweet professor.  Hurd is very passionate about the subject (environment) which really translated well in class.  She was always there for her students and was open to suggestions.  I would definitely take another class with her!

Great professor. She takes an interdisciplinary approach to Torts (Philosophy) but still covers the legal rules you need. Does not place a huge emphasis one cases, preferring to even tell the class the facts of a case rather than call on someone for them. You'll have to switch up your approach for her course but it's worth the effort.

Most useless professor at Illinois. Teaches the philosophy of torts instead of the law. Class is a waste of time. Doesn't use the case methods. A gunner's dream because she lets people ramble on about nothing for entire class periods

Hurd is amazing. I had ZERO interest in criminal law going in and thought I'd hate the class, but it turned out to be the most enjoyable experience of 1L year. She's amazingly bright, genuinely interested in ideas, and open to ways of thinking from other disciplines like empirical studies (and brings those into class).
she is perhaps the best teacher I have had thus far, and possibly during my entire academic career. She is the best.

Prof. Hurd is extremely charismatic, brilliant, and fun.  Students who are less comfortable with philosophy find the class difficult, but I enjoyed it immensely.  She really makes you think about the tough questions, about why the law should be or is one way or another. Outside of class, I found her approachable and willing to talk.  Loved it!

Professor Hurd can be very polarizing.  If you'd rather read and discuss an essay on legal ethics than memorize and regurgitate black letter law outlines, then you'll love her.  If your 3 years of law school are an unfortunate prerequisite to whatever you see in becoming a lawyer, then you'll probably dislike her demanding and subtle class.

High interest class - Hurd is a compelling presenter. Her Socratic method is not intended to embarrass and she hits several students per class rather than 1 or 2. She will do her best to make your answer forward the discussion. Supplements are less helpful, but still necessary (E&E). Heavy on judges/theory. Final was very fair, fun and difficult.

Hurd is as interesting as she is brilliant (very).  She talks quickly and her lectures can get confusing at times but she is always available to the students and genuinely looking to help them out.  She's great.
Professor Hurd is brilliant. She may explain things too fast or in a way that is hard for 1Ls to understand, but she is always available for office hours and makes an effort with each student

Professor Hurd's background is philosophy. If you want to learn the nuts and bolts - IE, if you can't wait for the bar/bri class to learn the technical points of a crime - then many of the other posters are right, you're going to be disappointed. Of course, if you're interested in the REASON the law is the way it is... Dean Hurd was amazing.

I would have been very confused without commercial outlines... but she was incredibly friendly and enjoyable on a daily basis. Visiting her office hours was one of the best things I have ever done. She's really helpful and willing to go over things a thousand times if you ask. Overall just a very genuinely nice and interesting person.

Dean Hurd was incredibly disrespectful and made a point of humiliating most people she called on.  She didn't teach torts; she taught philosophy of torts.  Her exam was also fairly unfair, in that the multiple choice portion included things never discussed in class or readings.

She teachers her opinion of the law, not the law.  Doesn't actually listen to what you say when you are called on.  You are called merely as a formality to pretend that she uses the socratic method.  She will call on you and then not listen to your answer.  Once you finish talking she says what she wanted to say the entire time.

Prof. Hurd, who is the law school's Dean, is a great dean but a CRAPPY professor.  I had her for Torts and she spent a good percentage of the course teaching philosophy of torts as opposed to actual torts of assault/battery, false imprisonment, etc.  Unfortunately, you can't "opt-out" of taking her.
aight
If you have her as a one-L, buy a really good study aid and read it before you get to her class.

Hurd is an awful crim law professor.  We know more about where she has vacationed and the "important" people she knows than we do about criminal law.  Here philosophical discourse has no use in practice and I get the impression she is trying to impress a certain Michael Moore by teaching a black letter law subject her completely abstract manner.
Energy is amazing!  She makes an effort to schedule time for office hours.  Exam was interesting...  Grading is pretty fair...  I think I was lost during almost all of the class discussions.
Tough class with a ton of theory and not much practice.
I don't know anything about torts, but I have memorized the curves of her body...
Very good professor.  Philosophical leanings but very interesting on the whole.  Easy to outline and fair examination.
her teaching style is conducive to learning.
Engaging class. A little paranoid re: the power cables.

Dean Hurd, while somewhat philosophical is always clear.  She speaks precisely and is very careful to have office hours to explain things that we didn't understand.  While the class does cover much philosophy of law, this gave the concepts a broader context and helped to explain the rationale for some esoteric torts concepts.

By far the most challenging and engaging first year law course and professor - some think she's too difficult, some think she has a huge ego.  She does and rightfully so.  Dean Hurd is one of the most articulate and eloquent speakers from whom I've ever had the pleasure of learning.

I thought Heidi Hurd was very entertaining. You had to think a lot in her class, so maybe that's what people didn't like.  But I think the exam was very straightforward.  She loves her philosophy though.

I personally love Dean Hurd. She is hard to understand sometimes, but she always lets you come to office hours to clarify. And her stories make class a little more interesting. Who else do you know who has climbed Mt. Kilamanjaro??
Professor is self laudatory and continuously covers philosophical material not applicable to the practice of criminal law.
All she does is try to prove to you how much smarter she is than you are.  It's not really that hard since she has been doing this crap for decades and we just started.  ****.
Much bigger than her head is her ego.  Her class doesn't offer any unique insights into philosophy, but it does offer alot in terms of her personal life, her achievements, her, her, her...

she is a genuinely brilliant individual, so much so that it might be her biggest drawback. capable of spinning a class off into the abyss of hypotheticals. also spends little to know time on black letter stuff then tests substantially on it.
Hurd has favorites and will call on the same 5 students every day. Not that I minded! She's very etherial and cares deeply about the philosophy behind every single legal rule.

Professor Robbennolt is the bomb. If you get her as your Torts prof., you have been blessed. Like, I hate math and don't care about empirical research, but she made me consider taking Empirical Methods in Law because she's the prof. for that class. She is that awesome. Just know that the exam will kick your rear end no matter how much you prepare.
Great lecturer and made the class fun. Would highly recommend.

Robbennolt is awesome. Torts was my favorite class as a 1L. It was the only class I actually wanted to participate in. She's passionate about teaching and is great at answering questions. Surprisingly difficult final.
Professor Robbennolt has a passion for teaching. Her style is direct and clear. She inspires her students' participation in a constructive and inspiring manner. She is the ideal law school professor.
Robbennolt is an amazing professor! Take advantage of the practice problems she gives.

Professor Robbennolt is wonderful. She cares a LOT about students' success. You will be very lucky to get placed in her section! Don't be deceived by how enjoyable Torts is, though... the final is by far one of the hardest ones I've had in law school.
Robbennolt is an amazing professor who I loved having. If you get a class she teaches count yourself lucky.
reviews.rating_tags: Gives good feedback--Clear grading criteria--Would take again

reviews.comment: Prof. Robbennolt was very clear, and easy to follow all semester. These were my favorite class discussions as a 1L.

Unfortunately this was a boring class. But Professor Robbennolt is very nice, extremely patient and helpful to everyone. She is very knowledgeable and presents information in a clear and organized manner.

Awesome Prof. extremely clear about the material and what you need to know for the exam. Do not underestimate her exam though, it was the hardest one I had my first semester by far. The MC questions are extremely difficult, make sure you know the jurisdictional splits or you will be screwed. Very nice person inside and outside of class!
Very good, very clear professor. Also a very nice and helpful person both inside and outside of class.

Genuinely friendly and affable with a great sense of humor. Her voice is rather grating but it's helpful because she's more voluminous when about to say something of import. She's very clear about what you need to know and doesn't humiliate you when you're called on. Very approachable outside of class. Highly recommended.
Her voice is obnoxious as her tone oscillates from normal speech to screeching and yelling at random intervals, but she's clear and on-point for what you need to know
Robbennolt's ADR class was ok.  It had a lot of stupid exercises and discussions, but at the end of the semester, you'll learn a fair amount of tactics.

Jason Mazzone is a very difficult professor to recommend. He requires students to purchase multiple supplemental materials in addition to the textbook. In class, he frequently uses cold calls in a way that feels shaming rather than pedagogical, and his manner be rude and dismissive. His exams seem designed more to showcase his own cleverness.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Really cool guy. He might come across as intimidating, but he genuinely loves this stuff and just wants to engage with you. Wish I realized that sooner! Only quirk is that 80% ish of his cold calls are to men only. Several guys even got cold called multiple times throughout the semester, while most women never got cold called the entire semester.

I do not think that the participation grade adjustment, which is very significant at up to +-2/3 of a letter grade, was administered in anything resembling an objective manner. Instead, I suspect those few who happened to gain his favor on certain days got a large increase in their grade.

I am studying for the bar and Mazzone prepared us the least out of all our professors. Without Barbri, I would be lost on Con Law. Mazzone seems more interested in torturing his students with cold calls, excessive reading assignments, and a final exam three times longer than the regular law school exam, rather than actually teaching.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Prof Mazzone is knowledgeable but his class is confusing if you don't know the ins and outs of the entire constitution. He also assigns A LOT of reading- its very overwhelming. BUT his exam, even though it was 8 hours long, was not too bad and I'm pretty sure there was a huge curve. Pay attention to the main ideas brought up in class.
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He does not have a powerpoint nor uses a blackboard. You just stare at his face all semester. I learned a lot, yes, but he could have put in more effort. There is like 50 pages of reading per class. The exam was okay, but as the previous poster pointed out, it was 8 hours....
Insane amount of reading. Didnt really learn anything from him. Class is wasted and used as a discussionary period. Super smart guy but could have structured the class better. Exam was 8 hours long.

Mazzone is absolutely awesome. I was completely dreading Con Law going into it, but it actually turned out to be my favorite class this semester. His lectures are really good, but you better be prepared to get cold called, and once you do get called, be ready to talk with him for 20 minutes about the case. Super smart and entertaining!
reviews.rating_tags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.--Amazing lectures
